simple_types.name: Iterable_Multiset
simple_types.code:
type Iterable_Multiset is private
with
  Iterable =>
    (First       => First,
     Has_Element => Has_Element,
     Next        => Next,
     Element     => Element);

simple_types.name: Multiset
simple_types.code:
type Multiset is private
with
  Default_Initial_Condition => (SPARKlib_Full => Is_Empty (Multiset)),
  Iterable                  =>
    (First       => Iter_First,
     Next        => Iter_Next,
     Has_Element => Iter_Has_Element,
     Element     => Iter_Element),
  Aggregate                 =>
    (Empty => Empty_Multiset, Add_Named => Aggr_Include),
  Annotate                  =>
    (GNATprove, Container_Aggregates, "Predefined_Maps");

documentation.description:
Multisets are empty when default initialized.
"For in" quantification over multisets should not be used.
"For of" quantification over multisets iterates over elements.
Occurrences in a multiset are counted modulo equivalence, the whole
equivalence class is included/excluded at once. As equivalence classes
might be infinite, quantification over elements of a finite multiset
could be infinite. Thus, quantified expressions cannot be executed and
should only be used in disabled ghost code. This is enforced by using
the SPARKlib_Full assertion level.

subprograms.code: function "+" (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset) return Multiset
function "-" (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset) return Multiset
function "<=" (Left, Right : Multiset) return Boolean

documentation.returns: Left <= Right if all the elements occur less in Left than in Right

subprograms.code: function "=" (Left, Right : Multiset) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Two Multiset are equal if and only if all the Element_Type have the same
number of occurences for both Multisets (possibly 0).

subprograms.name: Add
subprograms.code: function Add (Container : Multiset; Element : Element_Type) return Multiset

documentation.description:
Returns Container with the number of occurences of the equivalence class
of Element incremented by one.

subprograms.name: Add
subprograms.code:
function Add
  (Container : Multiset; Element : Element_Type; Count : Big_Positive)
   return Multiset

documentation.description:
Returns Container with the number of occurences of the equivalence class
of Element incremented by Count.

subprograms.name: Cardinality
subprograms.code: function Cardinality (Container : Multiset) return Big_Natural

documentation.returns:
The Cardinality of a Multiset is the number of equivalence classes in
the Multiset taking into account the number of occurences of these
equivalent classes.

subprograms.name: Choose
subprograms.code: function Choose (Container : Multiset) return Element_Type

documentation.returns: Returns an element of the Multiset

subprograms.name: Contains
subprograms.code:
function Contains
  (Container : Multiset; Element : Element_Type) return Boolean

documentation.returns: Returns True iff Element occurs at least one in Container

subprograms.name: Copy_Element
subprograms.code: function Copy_Element (Item : Element_Type) return Element_Type

documentation.description:
Elements of containers are copied by numerous primitives in this
package. This function causes GNATprove to verify that such a copy is
valid (in particular, it does not break the ownership policy of SPARK,
i.e. it does not contain pointers that could be used to alias mutable
data).

subprograms.name: Default_Count
subprograms.code: function Default_Count return Big_Natural

subprograms.name: Difference
subprograms.code: function Difference (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.returns:
Returns the difference of Left and Right, in which the number of
occurences of an element E is the difference between its number of
occurences in Left and its number of occurences in Right.

subprograms.name: Element
subprograms.code:
function Element
  (Iterator : Iterable_Multiset; Cursor : Multiset) return Element_Type

documentation.description:
The next element to be considered for the iteration is the result of
choose on Cursor.

subprograms.name: Empty_Multiset
subprograms.code: function Empty_Multiset return Multiset

subprograms.name: Equal_Except
subprograms.code:
function Equal_Except
  (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset; Element : Element_Type) return Boolean

documentation.returns:
Check if all the elements of Left except the equivalence class of
Element have the same number of occurences in Left and in Right and
conversely.

subprograms.name: First
subprograms.code: function First (Iterator : Iterable_Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.description:
In the first iteration, the cursor is the multiset associated with
Iterator.

subprograms.code: function Get_Multiset (Iterator : Iterable_Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.description: Retrieve the multiset associated with an iterator

subprograms.name: Has_Element
subprograms.code:
function Has_Element
  (Iterator : Iterable_Multiset; Cursor : Multiset) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Return True on non-empty sets which can be reached by iterating over
Container.

subprograms.name: Intersection
subprograms.code: function Intersection (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.returns: Returns the intersection of Left and Right

subprograms.name: Is_Empty
subprograms.code: function Is_Empty (Container : Multiset) return Boolean

documentation.description:
A Multiset is empty if it has no element. In other words, if the number
of occurence of all the elements is 0.

subprograms.name: Nb_Occurence
subprograms.code:
function Nb_Occurence
  (Container : Multiset; Element : Element_Type) return Big_Natural

documentation.returns: Get the number of occurence of Element in the Container

subprograms.name: Next
subprograms.code:
function Next
  (Iterator : Iterable_Multiset; Cursor : Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.description:
At each iteration, remove the equivalence class of considered element
from the Cursor set.

subprograms.name: Remove
subprograms.code:
function Remove
  (Container : Multiset; Element : Element_Type; Count : Big_Positive := 1)
   return Multiset

documentation.description:
Returns Container with the number of occurences of the equivalence class
of Element decremented by Count.

subprograms.name: Remove_All
subprograms.code:
function Remove_All
  (Container : Multiset; Element : Element_Type) return Multiset

documentation.description: Returns Container with no occurences of the equivalence class of Element

subprograms.name: Sum
subprograms.code: function Sum (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.description:
Returns the sum of Left and Right, in which the number of occurences of
an element E is the sum of its number of occurences in Left and its
number of occurences in Right.

subprograms.name: Union
subprograms.code: function Union (Left : Multiset; Right : Multiset) return Multiset

documentation.returns:
Returns the union of Left and Right, i.e. the smallest Multiset
containing both Left and Right.

subprograms.name: Valid_Subset
subprograms.code:
function Valid_Subset
  (Iterator : Iterable_Multiset; Cursor : Multiset) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns True on all multisets which can be reached by iterating over
Container.

generic_instantiations.name: Eq_Elements_Checks
generic_instantiations.code:
package Eq_Elements_Checks is new
  SPARK.Containers.Parameter_Checks.Equivalence_Checks
    (T                   => Element_Type,
     Eq                  => Equivalent_Elements,
     Param_Eq_Reflexive  => Equivalent_Elements_Reflexive,
     Param_Eq_Symmetric  => Equivalent_Elements_Symmetric,
     Param_Eq_Transitive => Equivalent_Elements_Transitive);

documentation.description:
Check that the actual parameter for Equivalent_Elements is an
equivalence relation.

This constant should only be set to False when no particular handling
of equivalence over keys is needed, that is, Equivalent_Keys defines a
key uniquely.

formals.name: Equivalent_Elements
formals.code:
function Equivalent_Elements
  (Left : Element_Type; Right : Element_Type) return Boolean
